title: California Housing Snapshot
content:
# California Housing Snapshot

## Summary
This starter knowledge document captures official California housing context that can support retrieval-augmented answers about the statewide market environment.

## Key Facts
- California's population estimate was 39,355,309 as of July 1, 2025.
- California had 14,877,904 housing units as of July 1, 2024.
- The owner-occupied housing unit rate was 55.9% in 2020-2024.
- The median value of owner-occupied housing units was $734,700 in 2020-2024.
- Median selected monthly owner costs with a mortgage were $2,946 in 2020-2024.
- Median selected monthly own

title: California Housing Snapshot
content:
2020-2024.
- Median selected monthly owner costs with a mortgage were $2,946 in 2020-2024.
- Median selected monthly owner costs without a mortgage were $829 in 2020-2024.
- Median gross rent was $2,036 in 2020-2024.
- Building permits totaled 101,545 in 2024.
- California averaged 2.84 persons per household in 2020-2024.

## Advisory Use
- Use this document when answering broad statewide affordability, ownership-cost, rent, or housing-supply questions.
- This is statewide context and should not be treated as a city-level comp set.

## Sources
- https://www.census.gov/quickfacts/california

title: California Population and Housing Estimates
content:
# California Population and Housing Estimates

## Summary
This document summarizes official California state demographic estimate sources that can help explain housing demand pressure, population shifts, and broad supply context.

## Key Facts
- The California Department of Finance publishes January population and housing estimates for cities, counties, and the state.
- The Department of Finance provides E-1 annual population and housing estimates with annual percent change.
- The Department of Finance also provides E-5 population and housing estimates for cities, counties, and the state acros

title: California Population and Housing Estimates
content:
- The Department of Finance also provides E-5 population and housing estimates for cities, counties, and the state across recent years.
- These sources are useful for tracking whether local housing demand may be supported by population and housing-stock changes over time.

## Advisory Use
- Use this document when an answer needs official California estimates rather than model-internal feature importance alone.
- These estimates are useful for contextual explanations about demand, supply pressure, and demographic trend direction.

## Sources
- https://dof.ca.gov/Forecasting/Demographics/Estimat

title: California Population Projections
content:
# California Population Projections

## Summary
This document summarizes official California population projection resources that can support long-range housing demand commentary.

## Key Facts
- The California Department of Finance Demographic Research Unit publishes state and county population projections through 2070.
- The published baseline includes total population projections, age breakdowns, sex breakdowns, and county projections.
- These projections are useful for long-horizon advisory responses, such as where sustained demand may matter more than a single-year price estimate.

## Adv

title: California Population Projections
content:
ng-horizon advisory responses, such as where sustained demand may matter more than a single-year price estimate.

## Advisory Use
- Use this document when questions ask about future housing pressure, long-term demand, or population-driven planning context.
- Projection outputs should be treated as planning context, not direct transaction-price evidence.

## Sources
- https://dof.ca.gov/Forecasting/Demographics/projections/
